title: Hello from the second brain
excerpt: First note pushed from my Obsidian vault to karljde.com — testing the new publish pipeline.
html:
This is the first note flowing from my Obsidian vault out to karljde.com.

I built a small publish pipeline that scans this vault for any note with publish: true in its frontmatter, converts the markdown to HTML, copies any referenced media into the site repo, and writes a single data/notes.json that the site renders.

Why

The plan all along was for this 2nd brain to feed a public personal-brand presence. Most of my thinking will stay private — daily notes, work decisions, family things — but a small percentage gets surfaced here: things I'm learning about automation, freelancing, and building systems for small businesses.

What gets published

  • Anything tagged publish: true in the frontmatter.
  • Tags from the frontmatter (minus publish) show up on the site for filtering.
  • The date defaults to the file's modified time, or whatever I put in frontmatter.

That's it. No build step, no platform — just node .publish/publish.js when I want to ship.
